documentation.value: Defines a message type, the fundamental unit of data in Protocol Buffers. Messages contain typed fields with unique numeric tags.
The name of the message type, in CamelCase by convention.
Field definitions with type, name, and unique tag number (1-536870911, excluding 19000-19999).
Defines an RPC service with method signatures. Used by gRPC and other RPC frameworks.
The name of the service.
The RPC method declarations within the service.
Defines an RPC method within a service. Supports unary and streaming (stream keyword) calls.
The name of the RPC method.
The request message type. Prefix with 'stream' for client streaming.
The response message type. Prefix with 'stream' for server streaming.
Defines an enumeration type. The first value must be zero and serves as the default.
The name of the enum type, in CamelCase.
Enum values with unique numeric assignments. First value must be 0.

signatures.label: oneof oneof_name { type1 field1 = tag1; type2 field2 = tag2; }

documentation.value: Specifies that at most one of the fields in the group will be set at a time. Setting one field clears the others.
The name of the oneof group.
The mutually exclusive field definitions. Cannot contain map or repeated fields.
Defines a map field. Key types can be any integral or string type. Value types can be any type except another map.
The key type: int32, int64, uint32, uint64, sint32, sint64, fixed32, fixed64, sfixed32, sfixed64, bool, string.
The value type: any scalar, enum, or message type.
The name of the map field.
The unique field tag number.
Defines a repeated field (array/list). Can contain zero or more values of the specified type.
The element type (any scalar, enum, or message type).
The name of the repeated field.
The unique field tag number.
Defines an optional field (proto3). Enables explicit presence tracking so you can distinguish between default and unset.
The field type.
The name of the field.
The unique field tag number.
Specifies the package namespace for the .proto file. Prevents naming conflicts between message types.
The package name, typically using dot-separated notation (e.g., com.example.project).
Imports definitions from another .proto file. Use 'import public' to transitively expose imports.
The path to the .proto file to import, relative to the proto path.
Specifies the Protocol Buffers syntax version. Must be the first non-comment line. Options: 'proto2' or 'proto3'.
The syntax version: 'proto2' or 'proto3'.
Sets a file-level, message-level, or field-level option. Common options include java_package, go_package, optimize_for.
The option name (e.g., java_package, go_package, optimize_for, deprecated).
The option value (string, integer, or identifier).
